summary: The repeated simulation failures and reflection analysis indicate that the 'err' output is being asserted for every cycle in S7, but the reference model expects 'err' to be asserted for only a single cycle immediately after the 7th consecutive 1 (i.e., on the transition into S7). To match the reference, the patch changes the output logic so that 'err' is asserted only when the FSM transitions from S6 to S7 (i.e., on the clock cycle after the 7th consecutive 1 is detected), not for every cycle in S7. This is implemented by tracking the previous state and asserting 'err' only when the previous state was S6 and the current state is S7. All other logic is unchanged.
risks: If the reference model expects 'err' to be asserted for more than one cycle (e.g., for every cycle in S7), this patch will cause mismatches.
If the testbench or reference model has additional corner cases for error signaling not described in the prompt, further mismatches may occur.
The use of prev_state introduces a one-cycle delay in tracking state transitions, but this is intentional to pulse 'err' only on S6->S7 transitions.
next_steps: Re-run simulation to confirm that 'err' is now asserted for only one cycle after the 7th consecutive 1, matching the reference.
Check that 'disc' and 'flag' outputs remain correct and unchanged.
If mismatches persist, review the reference model's exact error signaling behavior for further alignment.
